name: Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)
name_original: Tour por el Sendero de la Marina de Cope (SL-MU 23)
region: Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre
region_intro: Hiking in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre offers a diverse coastal and mountainous landscape in the Murcia region of Spain. The area features a 17-kilometer coastline with varied geological formations, including limestones, fossil dunes, dramatic cliffs, and secluded coves with black sand and slate. Mediterranean vegetation, including black juniper, covers the terrain, providing a unique environment for exploration. The region is characterized by an interplay of sea and mountain, creating abrupt and varied terrain suitable for hiking. Best hiking trails in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre The most popular hiking route is Cabo de Cope Trail (PR-MU 104), a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and offers views of the Cabo de Cope massif. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23), an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This trail follows the coastline with minimal elevation changes, suitable for a relaxed walk. Local hikers also love the La Galera Beach loop from Playa del Hoyo, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past beaches, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Hiking in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre is defined by its coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and Mediterranean scrubland. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal strolls to more challenging routes with elevation. The routes in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 300 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy coastal strolls to more challenging routes with significant elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The trails in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre feature a remarkable 17-kilometer coastline with diverse geological formations, including limestones, fossil dunes, dramatic cliffs, and secluded coves with black sand and slate. You'll experience a continuous spectacle of sea and mountain, with Mediterranean vegetation, including black juniper, covering the terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: Hikers can explore a variety of natural and historical landmarks. The rocky massif of Cabo Cope offers panoramic views, and you can visit the 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning vistas. You might also discover beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4858540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Women's Bath Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899539531/tour-along-the-cope-marina-trail-sl-mu-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile path that follows the coastline with minimal elevation changes, perfect for a relaxed walk with children.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil, almost pristine natural environment, the breathtaking coastal landscapes, and the unique ecological value of the park.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala de Calnegre – Women's Bath Beach loop from Cala Blanca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.4-mile trail with significant elevation gain, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre is a Special Bird Protected Area, so you might spot species like Bonelli's eagle, eagle owl, peregrine falcon, and trumpeter bullfinch. The park is also home to the black-headed tortoise and Iberian skink, and loggerhead turtles have been observed nesting in the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach loop from Playa del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.4-mile trail that takes you through coastal areas and past beautiful beaches. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower – Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint loop from Torre de Cope&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.5-mile route offering great views.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
A: The best time to hike is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), when temperatures are more moderate. Summer can be very hot, making longer hikes less comfortable, though coastal breezes can offer some relief.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at various access points and near popular beaches and attractions within the regional park. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route descriptions or local signage.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural parks in Spain allow dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage at the park entrance or specific trailheads for the most current rules regarding pets.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to secluded beaches or coves?
A: Absolutely. The park's 17-kilometer coastline is dotted with secluded coves and beaches. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach loop from Playa del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; will take you past beautiful coastal spots, and many other routes offer access to hidden gems like Cala de Calnegre and Baño de las Mujeres Beach.
tips: Wild beach within the Cape Cope Regional Park and Calnegre Points. It can be reached on foot or by bike along the GR-92 path that you can connect from Calabardina or at any point on the coast of the Cope beaches.
